ogTitle: St Helena vs St Helens Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Helena and St Helens Plains.
heading: St Helena vs St Helens Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Helena, Victoria and St Helens Plains, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Helena has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to St Helens Plains (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Helena or St Helens Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Helena has a flood risk score of 36/100, while St Helens Plains scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Helena scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. St Helens Plains scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Helena or St Helens Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Helena has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
